name: conversations_history
description: Get messages from the channel (or DM) by channel_id, the last row/column in the response is used as 'cursor' parameter for pagination if not empty

channel_id.description: ID of the channel in format Cxxxxxxxxxx or its name starting with #... or @...

include_activity_messages.description: If true, includes activity messages such as channel_join or channel_leave

limit.description: Limit of messages to fetch in format of maximum ranges of time (e.g. 1d, 1w, 30d) or number of messages (e.g. 50)

name: conversations_replies
description: Get a thread of messages posted to a conversation by channelID and thread_ts, the last row/column in the response is used as 'cursor' parameter for pagination if not empty

thread_ts.description: Timestamp of the parent message in format 1234567890.123456

name: conversations_add_message
description: Add a message to a public channel, private channel, or direct message (DM, or IM) conversation by channel_id and thread_ts.

payload.description: Message payload in specified content_type format

name: reactions_add
description: Add an emoji reaction to a message in a public channel, private channel, or direct message (DM, or IM) conversation.

emoji.description: Emoji name without colons (e.g. thumbsup, heart)

name: reactions_remove
description: Remove an emoji reaction from a message in a public channel, private channel, or direct message (DM, or IM) conversation.

name: attachment_get_data
description: Download an attachment's content by file ID. Returns file metadata and content (text files as-is, binary files as base64). Maximum file size is 5MB.

name: conversations_search_messages
description: Search messages in a public channel, private channel, or direct message (DM, or IM) conversation using filters. All filters are optional, if not provided then search_query is required.

filter_in_channel.description: Filter messages in a specific channel by ID or name

filter_users_from.description: Filter messages from a specific user

filter_date_before.description: Filter messages before a specific date (YYYY-MM-DD)

filter_date_after.description: Filter messages after a specific date (YYYY-MM-DD)

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-100)

name: users_search
description: Search for users by name, email, or display name. Returns user details and DM channel ID if available.

query.description: Search query - matches against real name, display name, username, or email

name: conversations_unreads
description: Get unread messages across all channels. With browser session tokens (xoxc/xoxd), uses a single API call for complete results. With OAuth user tokens (xoxp), scans a subset of channels per type.

channel_types.description: Channel types to scan (comma-separated: public_channel, private_channel, im, mpim)

limit.description: Maximum number of channels to scan

name: conversations_mark
description: Mark a channel or DM as read. If no timestamp is provided, marks all messages as read.

timestamp.description: Optional timestamp to mark as read

channel_types.description: Comma-separated channel types: mpim, im, public_channel, private_channel

name: usergroups_list
description: List all user groups (subteams) in the Slack workspace. User groups are mention groups like @engineering or @design that notify all members.

include_users.description: Include list of user IDs in each group

include_count.description: Include user count for each group

name: usergroups_me
description: Manage your own user group membership. Use action='list' to see which groups you belong to. Use action='join' to add yourself to a group. Use action='leave' to remove yourself.

action.description: Action to perform: list, join, or leave

usergroup_id.description: ID of the user group for join/leave actions

name: usergroups_create
description: Create a new user group (mention group) in the Slack workspace. After creation, use usergroups_users_update to add members.

name.description: Display name for the user group

channels.description: Comma-separated channel IDs to auto-add members

name: usergroups_update
description: Update a user group's metadata: name, handle (@mention), description, or default channels. Does NOT change members - use usergroups_users_update for that.

usergroup_id.description: ID of the user group to update

name: usergroups_users_update
description: Replace all members of a user group with a new list. WARNING: This completely replaces the member list - any user not in the 'users' parameter will be removed.

users.description: Comma-separated list of user IDs to set as members
